bugs.title: Three-dot menu popup clipped — cannot escape slide container overflow
bugs.description: On the My Lists screen (mobile), tapping the three-dot menu on a list item opens a dropdown that is clipped and not fully visible. The dropdown is cut off at the boundary of the slide container rather than rendering on top of surrounding content.
bugs.rootCause: The `.slideContainer` in App.module.css (lines 148-152) has `overflow: hidden`, which is needed for the iOS push/pop slide animations but also clips the absolutely-positioned `.menuDropdown`. A prior fix removed `overflow: hidden` from `.lists` in ListSelector.module.css, but the dropdown still can't escape `.slideContainer`. The DOM chain is: `.slideContainer` (overflow:hidden) → `.listScreen` → `.mobileFullScreen` → ListSelector `.lists` → `.listItem` → `.menuWrap` (position:relative) → `.menuDropdown` (position:absolute).
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport)
Log in and go to the Lists tab
Ensure at least one list exists
Tap the three-dot menu button on any list item
Observe: the dropdown menu is cut off / not fully visible
bugs.suggestedFix:
The `overflow: hidden` on `.slideContainer` is needed for slide animations but kills the dropdown. Best approaches:

**Option A (recommended for iOS feel): Render the menu as a mobile-style bottom sheet / action sheet.** On mobile, instead of a small positioned dropdown, render the menu options as a full-width fixed-position bottom sheet overlay. This sidesteps the overflow issue entirely and is the most native iOS pattern.

**Option B: React portal.** In ListSelector.jsx, render `.menuDropdown` via `ReactDOM.createPortal(dropdown, document.body)` so it escapes all overflow contexts. Position it using the menu button's `getBoundingClientRect()`.

**Option C: Toggle overflow on `.slideContainer` when menu is open.** Temporarily set `overflow: visible` when a menu is open. Risky if a slide transition fires while menu is open.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-dot menu options are fully visible when opened on any list item (first, middle, last)
Menu renders above all other content (proper z-index / stacking)
Slide animations (push/pop) still work correctly — no content leaking outside the container
No visual regression on the iOS grouped table view styling (rounded corners preserved)
Works on both mobile and desktop viewports
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
bugs.notes: A prior fix removed overflow:hidden from .lists — that was correct but insufficient. The real blocker is .slideContainer in App.module.css which also has overflow:hidden (needed for slide animations). The fix must work around it, not remove it.

bugs.title: Simplify mobile header — remove all controls and logo text
bugs.description: On mobile (≤700px), the header should be a slim, empty bar that serves as a container for contextual actions only (e.g., '+' for new list, share button on detail view). Currently it still shows the app logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out button. Username/ThemeToggle/Sign Out now live in the Settings tab. The logo text is unnecessary on mobile and wastes vertical space. Desktop header should remain unchanged (logo + username + ThemeToggle + Sign Out).
bugs.rootCause: The header JSX in App.jsx (lines 265-276) renders the logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out unconditionally with no mobile-specific conditional rendering. The `isMobile` hook is already imported but not used in the header.
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport) or resize browser
Log in with any account
Observe the header — logo, username, dark mode toggle, and Sign Out button are all visible
These controls are redundant with the Settings tab, and the logo wastes vertical space
bugs.suggestedFix:
In App.jsx, conditionally render header contents based on `isMobile`: ```jsx
{!isMobile && ( <>

ShoppingListAI

{isGuest ? 'Guest' : user.displayName ?? user.email}
)}
``` On mobile the header renders as a slim empty bar. Contextual actions ('+' new list, share) can be added to this header in a follow-up or within the individual tab/detail views as they are now. Alternatively, hide the entire header on mobile via CSS if no contextual actions are needed in it yet: ```css @media (max-width: 700px) { .header { display: none; } } ``` But keeping the header element (empty) is preferred so contextual actions can be slotted in later.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (≤700px), the header does not show the logo text, username, ThemeToggle, or Sign Out
On mobile, the header remains as a slim bar (not removed entirely) for future contextual actions
On desktop (≥701px), the header continues to show logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out (no change)
The Settings tab on mobile still works with dark mode toggle and sign out
No extra vertical space wasted on mobile — header should be minimal height when empty
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
bugs.notes: Combines two pieces of feedback: (1) username/ThemeToggle/Sign Out are redundant with Settings tab, (2) logo text wastes vertical space on mobile. The header bar itself should remain for future contextual actions but render empty/slim on mobile.

bugs.title: Mobile header still takes up vertical space — hide it completely
bugs.description: On mobile (≤700px), the header element still renders as an empty bar occupying vertical space even though its contents were removed in BUG-002. The header should be completely hidden on mobile via `display: none` so it doesn't waste any screen real estate. Desktop header remains unchanged.
bugs.rootCause: BUG-002 removed the header contents on mobile using conditional JSX (`{!isMobile && (...)}`), but the `<header>` element itself still renders. The ≤700px media query at line 246 sets `min-height: 0` and `padding: env(safe-area-inset-top, 0)` — but the ≤480px media query at line 273 overrides with `padding: 1rem 0 0.75rem`, adding visible vertical space for the empty header. Even without the 480px override, the empty element still takes up some space.
reproductionSteps: Open the app on mobile (≤700px viewport)
Log in with any account
Observe: there is an empty gap at the top of the screen where the header bar is rendered but contains nothing
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (≤700px), the header element is not visible and takes up zero vertical space
Safe-area-inset-top spacing is preserved for PWA/notched devices (applied to .app or another container if needed)
On desktop (≥701px), the header is fully visible with logo, username, ThemeToggle, and Sign Out (no change)
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
bugs.notes: Follow-up to BUG-002 which removed header contents but left the empty element visible.

bugs.title: Apple Sign-In redirects to localhost:3000 instead of production URL
bugs.description: After completing Apple OAuth consent in the live/production environment, Supabase redirects the user back to http://localhost:3000 instead of the production Vercel URL. The access token is present in the URL hash, so auth technically succeeded — but the user lands on a dead page.
bugs.rootCause: The Supabase project's **Site URL** in the dashboard (Authentication → URL Configuration) is still set to `http://localhost:3000` (or `http://127.0.0.1:3000`). This is the URL Supabase uses as the default OAuth redirect. The local `supabase/config.toml` also has `site_url = "http://127.0.0.1:3000"` and `additional_redirect_urls = ["https://127.0.0.1:3000"]` — but the remote dashboard setting is what matters for production.
reproductionSteps: Go to the production Vercel deployment of ShoppingListAI
Click 'Sign in with Apple'
Complete Apple's consent screen
Observe: browser redirects to http://localhost:3000/#access_token=... instead of the production URL
bugs.suggestedFix:
**This is a Supabase dashboard fix, not a code fix.**

1. Go to your Supabase project dashboard → **Authentication** → **URL Configuration**
2. Set **Site URL** to your production Vercel URL (e.g., `https://shoppinglistai.vercel.app`)
3. Add both your production URL and localhost to **Redirect URLs**:
   - `https://shoppinglistai.vercel.app` (production)
   - `http://localhost:3000` (local dev — so local dev still works)
   - `http://localhost:4000` (if using Vite dev port)
4. Save

Optionally, also update `supabase/config.toml` for local dev clarity, but the dashboard setting is what controls production behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: After Apple Sign-In on production, user is redirected back to the production Vercel URL (not localhost)
The access token is picked up by Supabase client and user is logged in
Apple Sign-In still works in local development (localhost redirect preserved in Redirect URLs list)
Email/password sign-in is unaffected
bugs.notes: This is a config-only fix in the Supabase dashboard — no code changes needed. The root cause is that site_url was never updated from localhost to the production domain after the Supabase migration. Production URL: https://shoppinglistai.vercel.app

bugs.title: New list does not appear after creation — requires page refresh
bugs.description: After clicking 'Create' to make a new list, the list does not appear in the UI. The user must refresh the page to see it. The list IS successfully inserted into Supabase (confirmed by refresh showing it), but the UI state doesn't update.
bugs.rootCause:
Two compounding issues:

1. **No optimistic update:** `createListAction` in ShoppingListContext.jsx (lines 191-195) inserts via `dbCreateList` and calls `setActiveListId(newId)`, but never adds the new list to the `lists` state array. It relies entirely on the Supabase Realtime subscription to deliver the new list.

2. **Silent Realtime subscription failures:** `subscribeLists` in database.js (line 158) calls `.subscribe()` with no status callback. If the channel fails to subscribe, enters CHANNEL_ERROR, or the Realtime event is silently dropped (due to RLS + expired JWT, network issues, etc.), there is zero error handling. The re-fetch inside the subscription handler also silently drops updates on failure (lines 120-137 — catch block logs but doesn't call the callback).

The result: `setActiveListId(newId)` points to an ID that doesn't exist in the `lists` array, so `activeListEntry` at line 130 returns `null`.
reproductionSteps: Open the app (any viewport)
Log in with any account
Click '+' or navigate to create a new list
Enter a name and click 'Create'
Observe: the new list does not appear in the list sidebar/selector
Refresh the page — the new list now appears
acceptanceCriteria: After clicking 'Create', the new list appears immediately in the list selector without a page refresh
The new list is selected (active) after creation
Realtime subscription failures are logged to the console (not silent)
If a Realtime event is missed, the optimistic update still shows the list
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
bugs.notes: The same silent-subscription pattern affects all 6 Realtime subscriptions in database.js (lists, items, history, stores, shared list refs, individual list). Consider adding status callbacks to all of them as part of this fix.

bugs.title: App gets stuck on 'Loading...' screen after refresh — requires multiple refreshes
bugs.description: After refreshing the page, the app frequently gets stuck on the 'Loading...' screen indefinitely. It eventually loads after refreshing several times. This is most noticeable on mobile/PWA and slower connections.
bugs.rootCause:
The `isLoading` state starts as `true` (AuthContext.jsx line 46) and is only set to `false` inside two async code paths that both lack proper error handling:

1. **No `.catch()` on `getSession().then()`** (line 50): If `getSession()` rejects (network error, corrupted localStorage token) or if the async callback inside `.then()` throws, `setIsLoading(false)` at line 55 never executes.

2. **`fetchProfile()` can throw/hang** (lines 52, 61): Both `getSession` and `onAuthStateChange` paths call `await fetchProfile()` before reaching `setIsLoading(false)`. The `fetchProfile` function (lines 16-28) handles Supabase query errors (returns null) but does NOT handle network-level failures (e.g., `TypeError: Failed to fetch`). If the network request hangs or throws, the error propagates and prevents `setIsLoading(false)` from running.

3. **No timeout fallback** (line 46): There is no mechanism to force `isLoading` to `false` after a maximum wait period. If both paths fail, the user is stuck forever.

4. **Async callback in `onAuthStateChange`** (line 59): Supabase doesn't await async callbacks, so if the callback throws after an `await`, it becomes an unhandled promise rejection.
reproductionSteps: Open the app in production (https://shoppinglistai.vercel.app)
Log in with any account
Refresh the page
Observe: the app shows 'Loading...' indefinitely (may need to try a few times, more likely on slower connections)
Refresh again — eventually it loads
acceptanceCriteria: After refreshing the page, the app loads within a reasonable time (under 5 seconds on a normal connection)
If fetchProfile fails (network error), the app still loads — showing the main content or login screen
If getSession fails, the app still loads — showing the login screen
The app never gets stuck on 'Loading...' indefinitely (timeout fallback)
No unhandled promise rejections in the console
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
bugs.notes: This is a critical UX issue — users may think the app is broken and abandon it. The fixes are relatively small (error handling + timeout) but have a big impact on reliability.

bugs.title: Cannot upload profile image on desktop — no click handler on header avatar
bugs.description: On desktop (≥701px), the header avatar is display-only. There is no way to change your profile photo from the desktop viewport because the MobileSettings component (which has the upload flow) is only rendered on mobile via the BottomTabBar settings tab. Desktop users have no settings page and no clickable avatar.
bugs.rootCause: The desktop header (App.jsx lines 293-312) renders the avatar as a plain or with no onClick handler, no hidden file input, and no upload logic. The profile image upload functionality only exists in MobileSettings.jsx which is exclusively rendered when isMobile is true (via the BottomTabBar settings tab). There is no desktop equivalent of the settings screen.
reproductionSteps: Open the app on desktop (≥701px viewport)
Log in with any account
Look at the header — there is an avatar circle next to your name
Try to click it — nothing happens
There is no settings page, gear icon, or other way to upload a profile photo on desktop
bugs.suggestedFix: Make the desktop header avatar clickable with the same hidden file input pattern used in MobileSettings.jsx: 1. Import `uploadProfileImage` from imageStorage.js and `useAuth` (for `refreshUser`) in App.jsx 2. Add `useState` for `isUploading` and `uploadError`, plus a `useRef` for the hidden file input 3. Add a `handleAvatarClick` that calls `fileInputRef.current?.click()` 4. Add a `handleFileChange` async handler that calls `uploadProfileImage` then `refreshUser` 5. Wrap the avatar ``/`` in a clickable container with `onClick={handleAvatarClick}`, `role='button'`, `tabIndex={0}`, `cursor: pointer` 6. Add a hidden `` next to the avatar 7. Show a small spinner on the avatar while uploading 8. Add a camera overlay icon on hover (matching the MobileSettings pattern but sized for 32px) 9. Show upload errors briefly (tooltip or inline text near the avatar)
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the desktop header avatar opens the native file picker
After selecting an image, it uploads and the avatar updates immediately
A loading indicator shows on the avatar during upload
A hover state indicates the avatar is clickable (cursor pointer + camera overlay)
Upload errors are shown to the user
The avatar remains display-only for guest users (no click, no overlay)
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
bugs.notes: The original PRD (US-003) explicitly stated 'The avatar does not act as a tap target on desktop'. This was a design decision, but in practice it means desktop users cannot upload a profile photo at all since there is no desktop settings page. The fix is to reverse that decision and make the avatar clickable on desktop too.
